title: acmella decumbens var. affinis
intro: Acmella decumbens var. affinis, commonly known as the parlor plant or toothache plant, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly to numb the pain and reduce inflammation associated with toothaches and other oral discomforts. The plant contains compounds such as spilanthol and acmellol, which are responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain, swelling, and discomfort. Herbal preparations of Acmella decumbens var. affinis can be used topically, often in the form of a tincture, oil, or poultice, to treat various oral health issues and alleviate toothache pain.
